phenotypes.label: Breast cancer and benign breast disease
phenotypes.description: The theme is female breast malignancy, frequently estrogen receptor–positive, accompanied by benign breast pathology (fibrocystic change, ductal hyperplasia) and local complications such as lymphedema and regional lymphadenopathy. Interstitial lung disease and eczematous skin disorders appear as associated comorbid conditions in some patients.

phenotypes.label: Diabetes with ocular complications
phenotypes.description: Diabetes, predominantly type 2, with prominent ocular microvascular complications: diabetic retinopathy (including proliferative forms) and macular edema, along with glaucoma, retinal detachment, amblyopia, and vision loss. Diabetic neuropathy and microalbuminuria co-occur, indicating broader microvascular disease, with episodes of hypoglycemia and hyperglycemia reflecting glycemic instability.

phenotypes.label: Neurogenic bladder/bowel with recurrent UTIs
phenotypes.description: Neurogenic bladder and neurogenic bowel, often with spina bifida and spinal deformity, complicated by recurrent urinary tract infections, bacteriuria, and E. coli isolation. Symptoms include dysuria and flank or coccygeal pain; seborrheic dermatitis and acne co-occur as dermatologic comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Secondary osteoporosis and fragility fractures
phenotypes.description: Pattern of osteoporosis/osteopenia with fragility fractures, prominently distal radius and carpal injuries. Secondary causes are represented, including primary hyperparathyroidism (parathyroid adenoma), renal osteodystrophy in end‑stage kidney disease with hyperphosphatemia, and hematologic/immune disorders such as multiple myeloma, myelodysplastic syndromes, immunodeficiency, and transplant‑related complications. This reflects bone fragility in older or immunocompromised patients, including postmenopausal/senile osteoporosis.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on cutaneous psoriasis with joint involvement (psoriatic arthritis/arthropathy), often including scalp disease. Co-occurring inflammatory skin and ocular surface disorders (rosacea, blepharitis, dry eye and visual symptoms) are prominent, reflecting dermatologic and ophthalmic comorbidity in this group.

phenotypes.label: Urologic cancers and lower urinary symptoms
phenotypes.description: Male urologic disorders predominate: benign prostatic hyperplasia with obstruction, erectile dysfunction, hematuria, and urethral stricture. Urologic malignancies are prominent as well, including bladder and kidney cancers, with related findings such as raised PSA, flank pain, renal cysts, varicocele, and inguinal hernia. Overall this reflects lower urinary tract symptoms and hematuria alongside urothelial and renal neoplasia.

phenotypes.label: HIV and sexually transmitted infections
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects HIV infection, including asymptomatic HIV, with prominent co-occurring sexually transmitted infections. Findings include syphilis, gonorrhea, genital herpes, and HPV-related anogenital disease such as condyloma, anal dysplasia, proctitis, and anal pain.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral arterial disease and aneurysm
phenotypes.description: Systemic atherosclerotic disease with peripheral artery involvement is prominent, with intermittent claudication, atherosclerosis of the extremities, and arterial aneurysms (abdominal and thoracic) including occasional dissection. Non‑rheumatic valve disease co-occurs in this vascular cluster. Dysphonia and vocal cord paralysis are seen, consistent with recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement from thoracic aortic pathology in some patients.

phenotypes.label: IBD with hidradenitis and atopy
phenotypes.description: This pattern centers on inflammatory bowel disease, dominated by ulcerative colitis and Crohn’s colitis, with Clostridioides difficile colitis as a gastrointestinal complication. It co-occurs with immune-mediated and atopic conditions including hidradenitis suppurativa, allergic conjunctivitis, sinonasal disease, and atopic dermatitis; musculoskeletal and airway features such as Achilles tendinitis and vocal cord dysfunction also appear.

phenotypes.label: Breast symptoms and galactorrhea
phenotypes.description: Patients show prominent breast-related signs including breast pain, nipple retraction, axillary mass, and other breast findings. Galactorrhea alongside pituitary adenoma points to a prolactin-driven endocrine cause of nipple discharge in part of this group. Other conditions appear but are less central to the pattern.

phenotypes.label: Menopause with migraine and sexual symptoms
phenotypes.description: This cluster reflects menopause and postsurgical menopause with diminished libido and sexual desire concerns alongside prominent migraine phenotypes, including aura and vestibular migraine. Associated comorbidities include vasomotor rhinitis and turbinate hypertrophy, autoimmune conditions such as celiac disease and psoriasis, and gynecologic findings like cervical lesions.

phenotypes.label: Ischemic stroke with sleep apnea
phenotypes.description: Cerebral artery occlusion dominates, indicating ischemic stroke, co-occurring with mixed sleep apnea and paroxysmal tachycardia. This reflects a cerebrovascular phenotype with sleep-disordered breathing and intermittent tachyarrhythmia, conditions that commonly cluster around stroke in older adults. Syphilis serology appears as part of neurologic evaluation in cognitive decline; other minor findings are incidental.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are ubiquitous chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, pain, nausea, GERD, anxiety), reflecting general baseline morbidity rather than a single syndrome. The more distinctive items are scattered across unrelated systems (traumatic injuries, ocular findings, ARDS, cellulitis, aspiration), indicating no coherent clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: No single clinical theme links the leading conditions. They mix very common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, GERD), pain and mood symptoms, and nonspecific complaints rather than a specific disease pattern.

phenotypes.label: Spine and myofascial pain syndromes
phenotypes.description: Chronic axial musculoskeletal pain centered on the spine, including low back and neck pain with frequent sciatica/radiculitis. Prominent myofascial pain and somatic dysfunction across cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, and pelvic regions, with muscle spasm and cervicogenic headache features. This reflects a chronic spine pain phenotype with radicular and myofascial components.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy and focal neurologic deficits
phenotypes.description: Prominent seizure and epilepsy diagnoses (generalized, localization-related, refractory) cluster with intracranial pathology and mass effect. Stroke, intracranial hemorrhage, hydrocephalus, and cerebral edema with focal signs such as aphasia, dysarthria, hemiparesis, ataxia, nystagmus, and dysmetria indicate seizures secondary to acute brain injury or cerebrovascular disease.

phenotypes.label: Lewy body dementia with RBD
phenotypes.description: Lewy body dementia with REM sleep behavior disorder is prominent, accompanied by parkinsonian features such as incoordination, abnormal posture, and balance impairment alongside cognitive deficits and attentional disturbance. Pelvic floor/autonomic dysfunction and restless legs are frequent, with iron metabolism disorders appearing as a related comorbidity seen in this synucleinopathy spectrum.

phenotypes.label: Prosthetic heart valve and valvular disease
phenotypes.description: Patients with structural valvular heart disease and a documented prosthetic heart valve, often with aortic valve findings. Other minor conditions (ocular surface injuries, rhinitis, musculoskeletal injuries) appear incidentally and do not alter the primary valvular theme.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by nonspecific acute complaints—pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and anxiety—along with scattered signs such as wheeze and rales. The conditions are common across many encounters and span multiple organ systems without a unifying disease process. It reflects general symptom coding rather than a distinct clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions lack a unifying clinical theme. It mixes a urinary tract infection term with unrelated ocular, musculoskeletal, dermatologic, and injury findings, so this signal is not clinically actionable.

phenotypes.label: Abdominal bloating and flatulence
phenotypes.description: Prominent functional gastrointestinal symptoms with gaseous abdominal distension, flatulence, gas pain, and passing flatus. Gynecologic symptoms (dysmenorrhea, irregular menses) co-occur in some patients, consistent with menstrual-related fluctuation of functional bowel complaints.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathy and neuralgia
phenotypes.description: Patients have peripheral neuropathy and neuralgia, including inflammatory neuropathies and hand mononeuropathies. Some have vitamin B12 deficiency, a known cause of neuropathy. Non‑neurologic items (e.g., peptic ulcer, skin lesions) likely represent unrelated comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy and gynecologic disorders
phenotypes.description: Female reproductive health pattern centered on pregnancy across trimesters with obstetric complications such as pre‑eclampsia, postpartum hemorrhage, premature labor, and high‑risk pregnancy. Co-occurring gynecologic conditions include pelvic pain, ovarian cysts, vaginitis/bacterial vaginosis, abnormal uterine bleeding, leiomyomas, and abnormal Pap smear; miscarriage and genitourinary bleeding are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Lower-leg fractures with neurologic sequelae
phenotypes.description: Cluster of lower-leg fractures (tibia/fibula) and their late effects (delayed union, stiffness, contracture) with chronic post‑injury pain. Co-occurring neurologic sequelae include difficulty talking/voice changes, sensory disturbance, and sequelae of intracranial injury or transient ischemia. This reflects fall-related trauma in older adults with combined orthopedic and neurogenic speech deficits.

phenotypes.label: Dermatologic conditions plus falls and trauma
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine unrelated dermatologic diagnoses (acne vulgaris, chloasma) with mobility and fall-related problems (impaired mobility, balance impairment, syncope) and associated injuries (compression fractures, hand lacerations, abrasions). There is no unifying clinical mechanism across these elements, so this is not a cohesive phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Back injury and abdominal wall hernia
phenotypes.description: Mechanical spine disorders dominate, with lumbar disc displacement and back injuries alongside related findings such as contusion, coccygeal disorders, retrolisthesis, and cervical arthritis. Abdominal wall hernias also appear, reflecting concurrent structural soft‑tissue defects; associated musculoskeletal conditions like patellar tendinitis and joint findings round out the pattern.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The conditions listed are generic symptoms and common chronic comorbidities (pain, chest pain, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, reflux, anxiety) without a unifying clinical mechanism. Additional outlier findings are scattered injuries and unrelated organ‑specific issues, so there is no coherent clinical theme to identify a patient group.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine aortic valve disorder with malignant lymphoma, plus abnormal mammogram and a thyroid nodule, spanning unrelated organ systems. There is no single clinical mechanism tying these together, so this does not represent a unified phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Age-related cataract and vitreoretinal disease
phenotypes.description: Ocular disease centered on age‑related nuclear cataracts with accompanying vitreous degeneration/posterior vitreous detachment and retinal tears or detachment. Ocular hypertension and glaucoma occur alongside myopia and macular degeneration, reflecting degenerative eye disease in older adults.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine a neurologic/spine pattern (foot drop, lumbosacral radiculopathy, post‑laminectomy syndrome) with unrelated findings such as cervical cytology/HPV results, lactose‑related malabsorption, bipolar disorder/substance use, and ocular complaints. There is no single clinical mechanism connecting these elements, indicating a merged set of unrelated issues.

phenotypes.label: Macular and retinal disease with cataracts
phenotypes.description: Cluster of ophthalmic conditions centered on macular and vitreoretinal disease (epiretinal membrane, macular hole, lattice degeneration, drusen age-related macular degeneration) alongside cataracts and related complications. Anterior uveitis, blepharitis, and steroid-associated glaucoma suspect are present, with visual deficits such as central scotoma, transient visual loss, and hemianopia. This reflects age-related retinal pathology with cataract and inflammatory comorbidity contributing to impaired vision.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix common baseline comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, GERD, osteoarthritis) with scattered injuries and unrelated findings (wrist trauma, ocular injury, aspiration pneumonitis). There is no coherent clinical pattern to interpret or use for cohort selection.

phenotypes.label: Cutaneous neoplasms and pigmented lesions
phenotypes.description: Dermatologic conditions centered on pigmented nevi (including dysplastic nevi), keratoses (actinic/seborrheic), hemangiomas, and other benign skin lesions. Malignant entities such as cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma and melanoma also appear, alongside skin discoloration and hyperpigmentation, representing a spectrum from benign to malignant cutaneous disease.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar spectrum with suicidality
phenotypes.description: Severe mood disorder pattern centered on bipolar I and manic/hypomanic episodes with co-occurring anxiety, insomnia, and depressive features. Prominent suicidal ideation and behavior, overdoses, and associated injuries including burns indicate elevated self-harm and accident risk.

phenotypes.label: Postconcussion syndrome with neurobehavioral features
phenotypes.description: Pattern of concussion/mild traumatic brain injury with persistent postconcussion symptoms. Features include posttraumatic headache, dizziness, anxiety/PTSD, and attention disorders (ADHD/inattentive type), with ocular motor issues such as convergence insufficiency and exophoria. Documentation of head injury and intracranial injury sequelae supports ongoing post‑traumatic effects.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic symptoms (chest pain, dyspnea, nausea) mixed with scattered injuries and unrelated findings, without a unifying clinical theme. This pattern does not delineate a coherent condition profile within the cohort.

phenotypes.label: Psychotic disorders with suicidality
phenotypes.description: Psychotic-spectrum disorders with prominent hallucinations, delusions, disorganized thinking, and agitation. Mood comorbidity is common (major depression, mania, PTSD, insomnia) with high rates of suicidal and homicidal ideation. Extrapyramidal signs and polysubstance or cannabis dependence also appear.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and acute symptoms (hypertension, diabetes, chest pain, nausea, anxiety) without a unifying clinical story. The rare high-specificity items are scattered across unrelated body systems, so this is not a usable clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Systemic sclerosis with myositis overlap
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune connective tissue disease centered on systemic sclerosis/scleroderma with overlapping inflammatory myopathy. The pattern includes gastrointestinal dysmotility with diarrhea, calcium metabolism abnormalities suggestive of calcinosis, cardiac conduction disease with complete atrioventricular block, and non-scarring alopecia as an autoimmune comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Mixed urologic, dermatologic, thrombophilia cluster
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions include lower urinary tract obstruction from benign prostatic hyperplasia and urinary retention, alongside nail and finger infections (paronychia, cellulitis). It also highlights inherited hypercoagulable states (Factor V Leiden, prothrombin mutation) and hemochromatosis. These represent unrelated systems without a unifying clinical mechanism.
